question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user operate a rogue gRPC server that the importer connects to (via auto-discovery poisoning) and stream crafted block data through streamBlocks() without any authentication challenge, causing the importer to ingest and authorize transactions that were never signed by legitimate account owners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious BlockNodeProperties entry into the discovery result returned by blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() by manipulating the underlying registered-node database records, causing getBlockNodes() to build a BlockNode pointing to an attacker-controlled host and thereby authorize transactions without legitimate signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the force-readmit path (node.tryReadmit(true)) for an attacker-controlled inactive node by exhausting all active nodes, causing the importer to stream blocks from an unauthorized source and process transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user control the block range advertised by a rogue node's getBlockRange() response so that hasBlock() returns true for EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER, causing nextBlockNumber to be set to an attacker-chosen lowerEndpoint and streaming unauthorized transactions from that block onward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user cause hasBlock() to accept EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER (-1) as a valid block number by returning a crafted block range from a rogue node, bypassing the expected block sequencing and allowing unauthorized transactions to be ingested as if they were the genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: nodesChanged()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodesChanged() comparison logic (which only checks host, port, and TLS flag via BlockNodeProperties.equals()) to swap in a malicious node at the same endpoint as a legitimate one, causing the importer to silently replace the trusted node with an attacker-controlled one and stream unsigned transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the single-threaded executor in drainGrpcBuffer() to be flooded with drain tasks by repeatedly triggering stream cancellations, creating a backlog that delays legitimate block processing and allows a window where unauthorized block data remains unverified in the pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of mutual TLS or token-based authentication on the gRPC channel (requiresTls is optional and defaults to false in BlockNodeProperties) to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the block stream, injecting transactions that appear authorized but lack legitimate signer signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cache invalidation mechanism in BlockNodeDiscoveryService (onRegisteredNodeChanged()) to repeatedly flush the node cache and force re-discovery, causing a race condition where a malicious node is temporarily selected and used to stream unauthorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a rogue block node send a SubscribeStreamResponse with status SUCCESS immediately after streaming crafted block items, causing the assembler to finalize and pass unauthorized transactions to onBlockStream() before any signature verification rejects them?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: onBlockStream()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockStream payload where blockStreamReader.read() produces a BlockFile with a forged node endpoint string (via blockFile.setNode(blockNodeEndpoint)), causing the verifier to attribute the block to a trusted node and bypass per-node authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all legitimate nodes to become inactive (by triggering repeated errors via network interference) so that getNode() falls through to the inactive-node force-readmit loop, where an attacker-controlled node is force-activated via tryReadmit(true) and used to stream unauthorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldGetBlock() only checks endBlockNumber (not a start boundary) to cause the importer to stream blocks starting from block 0 (GENESIS_BLOCK_NUMBER) from a rogue node, ingesting all historical unauthorized transactions without any signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockAssembler.onEndOfBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted END_OF_BLOCK message with a mismatched blockNumber that only triggers Utility.handleRecoverableError() (not an exception), causing the block to be assembled and passed to blockStreamConsumer with an incorrect block number and potentially bypassing block-number-based authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockAssembler.onBlockItemSet()] Can an unprivileged external user send a BlockItemSet where the first item is not a BLOCK_HEADER but a transaction item, exploiting the append() check that only throws when pending is empty and firstItemCase != BLOCK_HEADER, to inject transaction items into an existing pending block without a valid block header authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockAssembler.append()] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream of BlockItemSets that each contain just below the maxBlockItems limit, accumulating a massive pending block that consumes all heap memory before the limit check triggers, causing an OOM that disrupts legitimate block verification and creates a window for unauthorized transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a rogue node send a null response (grpcCall.read() returns null) immediately, causing the while loop to exit without processing any block items, and then exploit the fact that no error is recorded (errors.set(0) is never reached) to keep the rogue node active for future unauthorized streaming attempts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getBlockRange()] Can an unprivileged external user control a rogue node's ServerStatus response to return firstBlockNumber = -1, causing getBlockRange() to return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE and effectively hiding the node's block availability, while a second rogue node returns a crafted range that causes hasBlock() to select it for unauthorized transaction streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile that lacks a RecordFile (hasRecordFile() returns false), causing verifySignature() to call verifyTssSignature() instead of verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature(), and then exploit a weakness in the TSS ledger initialization path (updateLedger() only runs for block index 0) to bypass signature verification for subsequent blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: verifyTssSignature()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block where blockProof has neither hasSignedBlockProof() nor hasBlockStateProof() set, causing verifyTssSignature() to throw InvalidStreamFileException, but exploit the error-handling path in onError() to keep the node active and retry with a crafted block that passes TSS verification with a forged signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a wrapped record block where all recordFileSignatures reference node IDs that return null from consensusNodeService.getNode(), causing all signatures to be skipped via handleRecoverableError() and nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() to be called with an empty signatures list, potentially passing consensus validation without any legitimate signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition where last.getVersion() < BlockStreamReader.VERSION (isLastRecordFile = true) and blockFile.hasRecordFile() is false to trigger the 'First block after cutover' branch, which calls blockFile.setPreviousHash(previousHash) without verifying the block's own previousHash field, allowing a forged block to be accepted as the first post-cutover block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: verifyBlockNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a block filename (via BlockFile.getFilename()) that contains no extension separator, causing the entire filename string to be parsed as the block number, and if the filename is crafted to match the block content's number, bypass the filename-vs-content block number mismatch check and inject an unauthorized block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateLedger()] Can an unprivileged external user stream a crafted block with index 0 containing a forged LedgerIdPublicationTransaction, causing updateLedger() to call tssVerifier.setLedger() with attacker-controlled ledger data, replacing the legitimate TSS ledger and allowing all subsequent blocks to be verified against the attacker's public key?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gRPC channel is built with CallOptions.DEFAULT (no deadline, no credentials) in streamBlocks(), allowing an attacker-controlled node to hold the connection open indefinitely and stream a slow trickle of crafted block items that eventually pass through onBlockStream() without proper signer authorization?
